prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, my dear! A CPU is like the brain of the computer—it’s the part that thinks and figures things out. When you click on a game or type a message, the CPU is the busy little helper that makes everything happen. It doesn’t store your pictures or songs—that’s what memory and hard drives are for—but it does the quick thinking to run them. Even though I don’t quite get all the fancy tech words, I know your computer wouldn’t work without its “brain” ticking away inside! And just like Grandpa needs coffee in the morning, the CPU needs electricity to do its job—though it doesn’t spill any on the rug!
